page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, fall into the average tier. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% tier, while large-block memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier.

The instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, which introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated CPU instances, though it offers a cost-effective resource density profile with 64.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 960 GB NVMe SSD. This configuration provides a balanced hardware profile for workloads requiring high single-thread performance and moderate multi-core throughput without the premium cost of fully dedicated compute resources.
This server is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database operations, and file compression tasks that benefit from fast single-core execution and local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, including stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. PassMark memory testing ranks in the top 25%. Given its shared CPU model, it offers a cost-effective balance of high memory capacity and fast local storage. This instance is well-suited for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose workloads that require strong single-thread execution and moderate multi-core capabilities.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g-960s features 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring high-speed single-core performance, substantial memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
64.0 GB system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications
